_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-21 Blood Glucose Testing (Revised 2/24/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
level_display: Procedure (All EMS Levels — MFR requires MCA selection)
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-21: Blood Glucose Testing — MDHHS Approved 2/24/2023
purpose: Prehospital blood glucose testing for patients with altered mental status or per protocol indication. MFR-level use requires MCA authorization. No contraindications. Results documented in ePCR.

sections.title: Indications
points: Altered mental status (AMS) of any cause
Per specific treatment protocol indication (e.g., Diabetic Emergencies, AMS, Stroke, Seizures, ROSC)

sections.title: Technique
points: Obtain and test blood glucose per glucometer manufacturer's instructions
Use appropriate lancet technique — fingertip or alternate site per device
Ensure adequate blood sample for accurate reading
Document result in ePCR with time of testing

sections.title: Provider Scope
points: EMT, Specialist, Paramedic: authorized to obtain blood glucose without MCA selection
MFR: requires MCA authorization (MCA selection) to perform blood glucose testing

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select whether MFR-level providers are authorized to perform blood glucose testing
options: Not authorized at MFR level — EMT and above only

deterioration_flags: Blood glucose < 60 mg/dL — treat per Diabetic Emergencies Protocol (oral glucose if swallow reflex intact / dextrose IV/IO or glucagon IM/IN per scope)
Blood glucose > 300 mg/dL — assess for DKA/HHNS; IV fluid and transport; consult Medical Control
scope_notes: EMT, Specialist, Paramedic: authorized without MCA selection
Document result and time in ePCR
Indications: AMS or per specific protocol requirement
